phrases: how do i get the next chapter
where do i get the next chapter

top_24h.caption:
You think the more you love her, the better the relationship will be, but that hasn’t worked because that’s not God’s design. On the contrary, it’s actually the biggest danger to a christian marriage.

Not dangerous in an obvious way. Dangerous because they give everything. They prioritize her above everything. They make her their peace, their security, their purpose. 

And they call it love.
But all she feels is fear.

Why?

Because when you make a woman your foundation, you’re hiring her. You’ve given her the job of being your God. And no human being was built to carry the weight of being someone’s everything… She will feel it even if she can’t name it. The suffocating pressure of knowing that your emotional state rises and falls with whether she’s happy.

Men wonder why the most devoted partners get left. It’s because devotion aimed at the wrong target destroys the thing it’s trying to protect.

Here’s the biblical truth: 

She was never meant to be your source. She’s a gift from your source.
And the moment you start valuing the gift more than the one who gave it, you’ve lost sight of the target, and eventually, you’ll lose the gift too.

So then what does a man built to love do? 

“Monogamy to God” is the framework that fixes this. It means that your deepest loyalty, your identity, your security… none of that lives in her. 

It lives in God. 

Which means she can disappoint you without destroying you. She can push back, or be distant, or go through her own hard season… and you remain sturdy. Focused on providing her the safety of knowing that you will love her, even when she is scared.

Understanding this is one thing.
Breaking the old patterns is where most Christian men stay stuck.

So if you’ve been struggling to save your marriage, and you want to learn how to turn this hard season into the fuel to deepen your relationship with your wife…

I help Christian men understand what God wants them to learn through one-on-one coaching, so they 
can feel at peace during conflict, 
feel grateful for their wife’s emotions, and never live in fear of being left again.

Comment “NEXT CHAPTER”
and I’ll DM you the first step.

top_30d.caption:
If you’re suffering after heartbreak, this is how you get peace. 
Let me explain. 

You wake up without urgency.
You move through the day without sharpness.
You’re productive, but not driven.

You pray, but it feels distant.
You work, but it feels heavy.
You train, but it feels mechanical.

And underneath it all is the same question you don’t say out loud:
“What am I actually moving toward?”

Most high-level men think this came from heartbreak.
That losing her is what broke them.

But that’s not the real loss.

What actually disappeared was the purpose, direction, and identity you unknowingly built around another human instead of God.

She didn’t just matter to you.
She became the reference point for your future.

And when that collapsed, so did the internal compass that used to pull you out of bed with clarity.

So you do what disciplined men do.

You stay busy.
You double down.
You stay productive.

And it works, briefly.

Until the quiet returns.
Until the drive doesn’t.
Until the peace that should meet you at night never shows up.

Here’s the truth most men never hear:

Healing doesn’t come from time.
It comes from realignment.

From letting the version of you that attached purpose to a person die,
so God can rebuild your identity on something unshakable.

That’s the work I do.

I help high-level Christian businessmen who don’t have the luxury of drifting rebuild their purpose in Christ,
so mornings start with direction,
your energy finally matches the responsibility you carry,
and you fall asleep with peace, knowing you lived that day the way you were meant to.

I recorded a 3-minute video breaking down the exact pattern I kept seeing after coaching men through this season,
why the thoughts keep looping,
and the shift that restores clarity and peace.

Comment “NEXT CHAPTER” and I’ll send it to you.

The painful truth that follows will hurt. But I changed my life and of you let it, it will change yours too. 

If the vision that once fueled you feels flat…
If you’re still performing at work, but it feels meaningless…

You need to hear this.
Most businessmen don’t lose their edge because they’re tired.
They lose it because something collapsed.

And in your case…
It wasn’t just the relationship.
It was the future you built around her.

You didn’t just love her.
You built a world in your head.

The marriage.
The family.
The shared wins.
The reason you were grinding so hard.

Your business wasn’t just about impact.
It wasn’t just about stewardship.
It was about building something worthy of her.

Protecting her.
Providing for her.
Becoming the man she would never leave.

And when she walked away…
It wasn’t just emotional pain.
It was the death of the future you were working toward.

And when the future dies,
so does the fuel.
That’s why the vision feels flat.
Because the vision isn’t rooted in the Kingdom.

It is still rooted in her.

Jesus said:
“But seek first the kingdom of God and His righteousness, 
and all these things will be added to you.” 
— Matthew 6:33

You didn’t seek first the Kingdom.
You sought first:
– The relationship
– The approval
– The security

That’s why when that imagined future collapsed,
your ambition collapsed with it.
That’s not burnout.

That’s misplaced worship.

Understanding this is one thing.
Rewiring the thought patterns that were built around that idol…
That’s where most Christian men stay stuck.

They know the truth.
But they don’t know how to retrain their mind in real time.
So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak…

If you’re tired of the negative thought spirals…
And tomorrow night you want to look back at this season and genuinely call it a blessing…

I help Christian businessmen uncover what God is teaching them through 1:1 coaching so 
they can move from heartbroken to peaceful, hurt to grateful,
by rebuilding their purpose and drive in Christ in only 60 minutes. 

Comment “NEXT CHAPTER”
and I’ll DM you the first step. A free video I made explaining
why you can’t stop thinking about her.

And if you’re still stuck thinking about her…
If your drive hasn’t come back…
If your mind keeps replaying what you lost…

It’s because you don’t yet understand what actually happened.

I recorded a short breakdown explaining why you can’t stop thinking about her, 
and how I’ve helped over 100 Christian businessmen uncover the exact belief that created that idolatry fast. 

In a single focused 60-minute session, men have identified the root belief that created the idol in the first place, 
and once that belief is exposed, the obsession begins to lose its grip.

So if you want to understand what’s really draining your drive, and see if working together makes sense…

You have been fighting the wrong thing.

The thoughts about her are not the problem. The replays, the loops, the checking her profile at 11pm — those are not the root. Those are the symptom. And you have been spending all your energy trying to kill symptoms while the root sits untouched.

This is why the thoughts keep coming back.

If you’re suffering after heartbreak, this is how you get peace. 
Let me explain. 

You wake up without urgency.
You move through the day without sharpness.
You’re productive, but not driven.

You pray, but it feels distant.
You work, but it feels heavy.
You train, but it feels mechanical.

And underneath it all is the same question you don’t say out loud:
“What am I actually moving toward?”

Most high-level men think this came from heartbreak.
That losing her is what broke them.

But that’s not the real loss.

What actually disappeared was the purpose, direction, and identity you unknowingly built around another human instead of God.

She didn’t just matter to you.
She became the reference point for your future.

And when that collapsed, so did the internal compass that used to pull you out of bed with clarity.

So you do what disciplined men do.

You stay busy.
You double down.
You stay productive.

And it works, briefly.

Until the quiet returns.
Until the drive doesn’t.
Until the peace that should meet you at night never shows up.

Here’s the truth most men never hear:

Healing doesn’t come from time.
It comes from realignment.

From letting the version of you that attached purpose to a person die,
so God can rebuild your identity on something unshakable.

That’s the work I do.

I help high-level Christian businessmen who don’t have the luxury of drifting rebuild their purpose in Christ,
so mornings start with direction,
your energy finally matches the responsibility you carry,
and you fall asleep with peace, knowing you lived that day the way you were meant to.

I recorded a 3-minute video breaking down the exact pattern I kept seeing after coaching men through this season,
why the thoughts keep looping,
and the shift that restores clarity and peace.

If the vision that once fueled you feels flat…

It wasn’t just the relationship.
It was the future you built around her.

You didn’t just love her.
You built a world in your head.

The marriage.
The family.
The shared wins.
The reason you were grinding so hard.

Your business wasn’t just about impact.
It wasn’t just about stewardship.
It was about building something worthy of her.

Protecting her.
Providing for her.
Becoming the man she would never leave.

And when she walked away…
It wasn’t just emotional pain.
It was the death of the future you were working toward.

And when the future dies,
so does the fuel.
That’s why the vision feels flat.
Because the vision isn’t rooted in the Kingdom.

It is still rooted in her.

Jesus said:

“But seek first the kingdom of God and His righteousness,
and all these things will be added to you.”

You didn’t seek first the Kingdom.
You sought first:

– The approval
– The security

That’s why when that imagined future collapsed,
your ambition collapsed with it.
That’s not burnout.

That’s misplaced worship.

Understanding this is one thing.

You’re not heartbroken because of the reason you think… And that’s why it feels like you can’t move on.
In 30 seconds I’ll explain biblically why you’re stuck, and how to actually start feeling peace again. 

The pain hits like a truck—and it stays.
You pray. You journal. You cry.

But the fog doesn’t lift.

I used to think the pain came from heartbreak.
I was wrong.

Let me show you the Biblical Truth that flipped everything:

“Now listen, you who say, ‘Today or tomorrow we will go to this or that city…’
Why, you do not even know what will happen tomorrow.
What is your life? You are a mist that appears for a little while and then vanishes.

Instead, you ought to say, ‘If it is the Lord’s will…’”
— James 4:13–15

You thought the pain came from what happened.
But what if it came from the story you wrote…
That God never signed?

The person you thought you’d marry.
The career you assumed was yours.
The future you believed God promised.

That version of life felt real.
But that future was only real in your imagination. Your best guess at what could be.
And when God chose a better future than the one you imagined…
You didn’t just lose what you wanted.

You lost your faith in His plan, and it was that mistake that stole your peace.
That’s why James says:

Don’t say, “I’ll do this or that.”
Say, “If it’s the Lord’s will.”

Surrender isn’t weakness.
It’s oxygen.

It’s stepping into the moment God is actually in…
Not the one you hoped He’d bless.

Understanding this is one thing.
Breaking the pattern is where most men stay stuck.

So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak and this helped you…

and you want to stop the negative thought spirals, break soul-ties, 
and actually be able to remember this heartbreak experience as the greatest gift you’ve ever received…

I help Christian businessmen understand what God wants them to learn
through one-on-one coaching, so they can feel grateful the relationship ended,
and never feel the pain of heartbreak again.

If you’re suffering after heartbreak, this is how you get peace. 
Let me explain. 

You wake up without urgency.
You move through the day without sharpness.
You’re productive, but not driven.

You pray, but it feels distant.
You work, but it feels heavy.
You train, but it feels mechanical.

And underneath it all is the same question you don’t say out loud:
“What am I actually moving toward?”

Most high-level men think this came from heartbreak.
That losing her is what broke them.

But that’s not the real loss.

What actually disappeared was something subtle, but vicious. I bet this sounds familiar…

She didn’t just matter to you.
She became the reference point for your future.

And when that collapsed, so did the internal compass that used to pull you out of bed with clarity.

So you do what disciplined men do.

You stay busy.
You double down.
You stay productive.

And it works, briefly.

Until the quiet returns.
Until the drive doesn’t.
Until the peace that should meet you at night never shows up.

Here’s the truth most men never hear: The pain you’re feeling comes from 
building your purpose, direction, and identity unknowingly 
around another human instead of God.

Which means healing won’t come from time.
It will come from realignment.

From letting the version of you that attached purpose to a person die,
so God can rebuild your identity on something unshakable.

So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak and this helped you...
and you want to stop the negative thought spirals, break soul-ties, 
and actually be able to remember this heartbreak experience 
as the greatest gift you’ve ever received…

I help Christian businessmen making 6-7 figures understand what God wants them to learn 
through one-on-one coaching, so then can feel grateful the relationship ended, 
and never feel the pain of heartbreak again.

If you’re suffering after heartbreak, this is how you get peace. 
Let me explain. 

You wake up without urgency.
You move through the day without sharpness.
You’re productive, but not driven.

You pray, but it feels distant.
You work, but it feels heavy.
You train, but it feels mechanical.

And underneath it all is the same question you don’t say out loud:
“What am I actually moving toward?”

Most high-level men think this came from heartbreak.
That losing her is what broke them.

But that’s not the real loss.

What actually disappeared was the purpose, direction, and identity you unknowingly built around another human instead of God.

She didn’t just matter to you.
She became the reference point for your future.

And when that collapsed, so did the internal compass that used to pull you out of bed with clarity.

So you do what disciplined men do.

You stay busy.
You double down.
You stay productive.

And it works, briefly.

Until the quiet returns.
Until the drive doesn’t.
Until the peace that should meet you at night never shows up.

Here’s the truth most men never hear:

Healing doesn’t come from time.
It comes from realignment.

From letting the version of you that attached purpose to a person die,
so God can rebuild your identity on something unshakable.

That’s the work I do.

I help high-level Christian businessmen who don’t have the luxury of drifting rebuild their purpose in Christ,
so mornings start with direction,
your energy finally matches the responsibility you carry,
and you fall asleep with peace, knowing you lived that day the way you were meant to.

I recorded a 3-minute video breaking down the exact pattern I kept seeing after coaching men through this season,
why the thoughts keep looping,
and the shift that restores clarity and peace.

You think the more you love her, the better the relationship will be, but that hasn’t worked because that’s not God’s design. On the contrary, it’s actually the biggest danger to a christian marriage.

Not dangerous in an obvious way. Dangerous because they give everything. They prioritize her above everything. They make her their peace, their security, their purpose. 

And they call it love.
But all she feels is fear.

Why?

Because when you make a woman your foundation, you’re hiring her. You’ve given her the job of being your God. And no human being was built to carry the weight of being someone’s everything… She will feel it even if she can’t name it. The suffocating pressure of knowing that your emotional state rises and falls with whether she’s happy.

Men wonder why the most devoted partners get left. It’s because devotion aimed at the wrong target destroys the thing it’s trying to protect.

Here’s the biblical truth: 

She was never meant to be your source. She’s a gift from your source.
And the moment you start valuing the gift more than the one who gave it, you’ve lost sight of the target, and eventually, you’ll lose the gift too.

So then what does a man built to love do? 

“Monogamy to God” is the framework that fixes this. It means that your deepest loyalty, your identity, your security… none of that lives in her. 

It lives in God. 

Which means she can disappoint you without destroying you. She can push back, or be distant, or go through her own hard season… and you remain sturdy. Focused on providing her the safety of knowing that you will love her, even when she is scared.

Understanding this is one thing.
Breaking the old patterns is where most Christian men stay stuck.

So if you’ve been struggling to save your marriage, and you want to learn how to turn this hard season into the fuel to deepen your relationship with your wife…

I help Christian men understand what God wants them to learn through one-on-one coaching, so they 
can feel at peace during conflict, 
feel grateful for their wife’s emotions, and never live in fear of being left again.

And they call it love.
But all she feels is fear.

Why?

Because when you make a woman your foundation, you’re hiring her. You’ve given her the job of being your God. And no human being was built to carry the weight of being someone’s everything… She will feel it even if she can’t name it. The suffocating pressure of knowing that your emotional state rises and falls with whether she’s happy.

Men wonder why the most devoted partners get left. It’s because devotion aimed at the wrong target destroys the thing it’s trying to protect.

Here’s the biblical truth: 

She was never meant to be your source. She’s a gift from your source.

You have been fighting the wrong thing.

The thoughts about her are not the problem. The replays, the loops, the checking her profile at 11pm — those are not the root. Those are the symptom. And you have been spending all your energy trying to kill symptoms while the root sits untouched.

This is why the thoughts keep coming back.

You block her, and a week later you are back on the page. You journal it out, and the loop starts again by morning. You pray against it, and it returns by Thursday. Not because you are weak. Because you are fighting the output and leaving the source running.

The source is idolatry.

Somewhere underneath the obsession, you gave her a role that only God can fill. Source of worth. Proof of value. Evidence that you are enough. And until that displacement is addressed, the thoughts will keep generating because the root is still being fed.

Kill the idolatry and the thoughts starve.

Not suppress them. Not white-knuckle through them. Starve them. Remove the thing feeding them at the root, and the symptom stops having anything to run on.

This is not a thought management problem. It is a worship problem. And worship problems have worship solutions.

Understanding this is one thing.
Rewiring the thought patterns that were built around that idol…
That’s where most Christian men stay stuck.

They know the truth.
But they don’t know how to retrain their mind in real time.
So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak…

If you’re tired of the negative thought spirals…
And tomorrow night you want to look back at this season and genuinely call it a blessing…

I help Christian businessmen uncover what God is teaching them through 1:1 coaching so 
they can move from heartbroken to peaceful, hurt to grateful,
by rebuilding their purpose and drive in Christ in only 60 minutes.

why you can’t stop thinking about her.

You have been fighting the wrong thing.

The thoughts about her are not the problem. The replays, the loops, the checking her profile at 11pm — those are not the root. Those are the symptom. And you have been spending all your energy trying to kill symptoms while the root sits untouched.

This is why the thoughts keep coming back.

You block her, and a week later you are back on the page. You journal it out, and the loop starts again by morning. You pray against it, and it returns by Thursday. Not because you are weak. Because you are fighting the output and leaving the source running.

The source is idolatry.

Somewhere underneath the obsession, you gave her a role that only God can fill. Source of worth. Proof of value. Evidence that you are enough. And until that displacement is addressed, the thoughts will keep generating because the root is still being fed.

Kill the idolatry and the thoughts starve.

Not suppress them. Not white-knuckle through them. Starve them. Remove the thing feeding them at the root, and the symptom stops having anything to run on.

This is not a thought management problem. It is a worship problem. And worship problems have worship solutions.

Understanding this is one thing.
Rewiring the thought patterns that were built around that idol…
That’s where most Christian men stay stuck.

They know the truth.
But they don’t know how to retrain their mind in real time.

Most men are trying to escape the very thing God is using to build them.

They treat pain like a problem to be solved.

They want it gone. They pray it away. They distract it away. And when it is still there on Tuesday morning, they conclude that something is broken, either in them or in God’s plan.

Here is what no one told you. The pain is not the problem. The pain is the equipment.

Think about a gym. You do not walk in hoping the weights feel lighter this week. You show up because resistance is the only thing that builds anything. Pull the weight. Feel the burn. Walk out stronger. That is not a punishment. That is the mechanism.

That is how strength works. And you think God operates differently?

When God allows heartbreak, loss, or a season that feels like silence, He is not withholding comfort from you. He is offering you a bar to press.

You have a choice every single morning. Go to the gym or go to the bar. Lift the weight of the cross or buy yourself another round of comfort and stay exactly the same.

“Skinny fat” is real. A man who avoids hard training but does not visibly look broken. Functioning. Fine. But soft underneath.

That is what spiritual avoidance produces. A man who looks fine, until life hits him.

The men who come out of painful seasons with clarity, with character, with settled confidence did not get lucky. They went to the gym. They let the pain do what pain is designed to do.

You cannot get spiritually strong on a comfortable plan.

So if your chest is heavy right now, good. That means there is weight on the bar.

“What then shall we say to these things? If God is for us, who can be against us?” Romans 8:31

You are not lifting alone.

The painful truth that follows will hurt. But it changed my life and if you let it, it will change yours too.

If the vision that once fueled you feels flat…
If you’re still performing at work, but it feels meaningless…

You need to hear this.
Most businessmen don’t lose their edge because they’re tired.
They lose it because something collapsed.

And in your case…
It wasn’t just the relationship.
It was the future you built around her.

You didn’t just love her.
You built a world in your head.

The marriage.
The family.
The shared wins.
The reason you were grinding so hard.

Your business wasn’t just about impact.
It wasn’t just about stewardship.
It was about building something worthy of her.

Protecting her.
Providing for her.
Becoming the man she would never leave.

And when she walked away…
It wasn’t just emotional pain.
It was the death of the future you were working toward.

And when the future dies,
so does the fuel.
That’s why the vision feels flat.
Because the vision isn’t rooted in the Kingdom.

It is still rooted in her.

Jesus said:

“But seek first the kingdom of God and His righteousness,
and all these things will be added to you.”
— Matthew 6:33

You didn’t seek first the Kingdom.
You sought first:
– The relationship
– The approval
– The security

That’s why when that imagined future collapsed,
your ambition collapsed with it.
That’s not burnout.

That’s misplaced worship.

Understanding this is one thing.
Rewiring the thought patterns that were built around that idol…
That’s where most Christian men stay stuck.

They know the truth.
But they don’t know how to retrain their mind in real time.

So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak…

If you’re tired of the negative thought spirals…

I help Christian businessmen uncover what God is teaching them through 1:1 coaching so
they can move from heartbroken to peaceful and hurt to grateful,
by rebuilding their purpose and drive in Christ in only 60 minutes.

The painful truth that follows will hurt. But it changed my life and if you let it, it will change yours too.

If the vision that once fueled you feels flat…
If you’re still performing at work, but it feels meaningless…

You need to hear this.
Most businessmen don’t lose their edge because they’re tired.
They lose it because something collapsed.

And in your case…
It wasn’t just the relationship.
It was the future you built around her.

You didn’t just love her.
You built a world in your head.

The marriage.
The family.
The shared wins.
The reason you were grinding so hard.

Your business wasn’t just about impact.
It wasn’t just about stewardship.
It was about building something worthy of her.

Protecting her.
Providing for her.
Becoming the man she would never leave.

And when she walked away…
It wasn’t just emotional pain.
It was the death of the future you were working toward.

And when the future dies,
so does the fuel.
That’s why the vision feels flat.
Because the vision isn’t rooted in the Kingdom.

It is still rooted in her.

Jesus said:

“But seek first the kingdom of God and His righteousness,
and all these things will be added to you.”
— Matthew 6:33

You didn’t seek first the Kingdom.
You sought first:
– The relationship
– The approval
– The security

That’s why when that imagined future collapsed,
your ambition collapsed with it.
That’s not burnout.

That’s misplaced worship.

Understanding this is one thing.
Rewiring the thought patterns that were built around that idol…
That’s where most Christian men stay stuck.

They know the truth.
But they don’t know how to retrain their mind in real time.

So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak…

If you’re tired of the negative thought spirals…

I help Christian businessmen uncover what God is teaching them through 1:1 coaching so
they can move from heartbroken to peaceful and hurt to grateful,
by rebuilding their purpose and drive in Christ in only 60 minutes.

You built a world in your head.

The marriage.
The family.

The reason you were grinding so hard.

It was the death of the future you were working toward.

so does the fuel.

and all these things will be added to you.”

– The approval
– The security

That’s why when that imagined future collapsed,
your ambition collapsed with it.
That’s not burnout.

That’s misplaced worship.

And if you’re still stuck thinking about her…

James says :

“Now listen, you who say, ‘Today or tomorrow we will go to this or that city…’
Why, you do not even know what will happen tomorrow.
What is your life? You are a mist that appears for a little while and then vanishes.

Instead, you ought to say, ‘If it is the Lord’s will…’”
— James 4:13–15

You thought the pain came from what happened.

But it came from the story you wrote ,
that God never signed.

You assumed.
You declared.
You built a five year plan
and called it God’s will.

When He chose something different
it didn’t just break your heart.

It exposed your attachment.

Surrender isn’t weakness.

It’s oxygen.

It’s stepping into the life God is actually in ,
not the one you hoped He would bless.

Most men don’t miss the woman.

They miss the version of themselves they thought they were becoming with her.

And until you kill that future
you’ll keep replaying it in your mind.

If you’re a Christian man who knows God allowed this,
but you still can’t stop thinking about her…

“Now listen, you who say, ‘Today or tomorrow we will go to this or that city…’

You thought the pain came from what happened.
But what if it came from the story you wrote…
That God never signed?

The person you thought you’d marry.
The career you assumed was yours.
The future you believed God promised.

That version of life felt real.
But that future was only real in your imagination. Your best guess at what could be.
And when God chose a better future than the one you imagined…
You didn’t just lose what you wanted.

You lost your faith in His plan, and it was that mistake that stole your peace.
That’s why James says:

Don’t say, “I’ll do this or that.”
Say, “If it’s the Lord’s will.”

Surrender isn’t weakness.
It’s oxygen.

It’s stepping into the moment God is actually in…
Not the one you hoped He’d bless.

Understanding this is one thing.
Breaking the pattern is where most men stay stuck.

So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak and this helped you…

and you want to stop the negative thought spirals, break soul-ties,

You’re not heartbroken because of the reason you think… And that’s why it feels like you can’t move on.
In 30 seconds I’ll explain biblically why you’re stuck, and how to actually start feeling peace again. 

The pain hits like a truck—and it stays.
You pray. You journal. You cry.

But the fog doesn’t lift.

I used to think the pain came from heartbreak.
I was wrong.

Let me show you the Biblical Truth that flipped everything:

“Now listen, you who say, ‘Today or tomorrow we will go to this or that city…’
Why, you do not even know what will happen tomorrow.
What is your life? You are a mist that appears for a little while and then vanishes.

Instead, you ought to say, ‘If it is the Lord’s will…’”
— James 4:13–15

You thought the pain came from what happened.
But what if it came from the story you wrote…
That God never signed?

The person you thought you’d marry.
The career you assumed was yours.
The future you believed God promised.

That version of life felt real.
But that future was only real in your imagination. Your best guess at what could be.
And when God chose a better future than the one you imagined…
You didn’t just lose what you wanted.

You lost your faith in His plan, and it was that mistake that stole your peace.
That’s why James says:

Don’t say, “I’ll do this or that.”
Say, “If it’s the Lord’s will.”

Surrender isn’t weakness.
It’s oxygen.

It’s stepping into the moment God is actually in…
Not the one you hoped He’d bless.

Understanding this is one thing.
Breaking the pattern is where most men stay stuck.

So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak and this helped you…

and you want to stop the negative thought spirals, break soul-ties, 
and actually be able to remember this heartbreak experience as the greatest gift you’ve ever received…

I help Christian businessmen understand what God wants them to learn
through one-on-one coaching, so they can feel grateful the relationship ended,
and never feel the pain of heartbreak again.

If you’re suffering after heartbreak, this is how you get peace. 
Let me explain. 

You wake up without urgency.
You move through the day without sharpness.
You’re productive, but not driven.

You pray, but it feels distant.
You work, but it feels heavy.
You train, but it feels mechanical.

And underneath it all is the same question you don’t say out loud:
“What am I actually moving toward?”

Most high-level men think this came from heartbreak.
That losing her is what broke them.

But that’s not the real loss.

What actually disappeared was something subtle, but vicious. I bet this sounds familiar…

She didn’t just matter to you.
She became the reference point for your future.

And when that collapsed, so did the internal compass that used to pull you out of bed with clarity.

So you do what disciplined men do.

You stay busy.
You double down.
You stay productive.

And it works, briefly.

Until the quiet returns.
Until the drive doesn’t.
Until the peace that should meet you at night never shows up.

Here’s the truth most men never hear: The pain you’re feeling comes from 
building your purpose, direction, and identity unknowingly 
around another human instead of God.

Which means healing won’t come from time.
It will come from realignment.

From letting the version of you that attached purpose to a person die,
so God can rebuild your identity on something unshakable.

So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak and this helped you...
and you want to stop the negative thought spirals, break soul-ties, 
and actually be able to remember this heartbreak experience 
as the greatest gift you’ve ever received…

I help Christian businessmen making 6-7 figures understand what God wants them to learn 
through one-on-one coaching, so then can feel grateful the relationship ended, 
and never feel the pain of heartbreak again.

so God can rebuild your identity on something unshakable.

That’s the work I do.

I help high-level Christian businessmen who don’t have the luxury of drifting rebuild their purpose in Christ,
so mornings start with direction,
your energy finally matches the responsibility you carry,
and you fall asleep with peace, knowing you lived that day the way you were meant to.

I recorded a 3-minute video breaking down the exact pattern I kept seeing after coaching men through this season,
why the thoughts keep looping,
and the shift that restores clarity and peace.

Comment “NEXT CHAPTER” and I’ll send it to you.

If you’re suffering after heartbreak, this is how you get peace. 
Let me explain. 

You wake up without urgency.
You move through the day without sharpness.
You’re productive, but not driven.

You pray, but it feels distant.
You work, but it feels heavy.
You train, but it feels mechanical.

And underneath it all is the same question you don’t say out loud:
“What am I actually moving toward?”

Most high-level men think this came from heartbreak.
That losing her is what broke them.

But that’s not the real loss.

What actually disappeared was the purpose, direction, and identity you unknowingly built around another human instead of God.

She didn’t just matter to you.
She became the reference point for your future.

And when that collapsed, so did the internal compass that used to pull you out of bed with clarity.

So you do what disciplined men do.

You stay busy.
You double down.
You stay productive.

And it works, briefly.

Until the quiet returns.
Until the drive doesn’t.
Until the peace that should meet you at night never shows up.

Here’s the truth most men never hear:

Healing doesn’t come from time.
It comes from realignment.

From letting the version of you that attached purpose to a person die,
so God can rebuild your identity on something unshakable.

That’s the work I do.

I help high-level Christian businessmen who don’t have the luxury of drifting rebuild their purpose in Christ,
so mornings start with direction,
your energy finally matches the responsibility you carry,
and you fall asleep with peace, knowing you lived that day the way you were meant to.

why the thoughts keep looping,
and the shift that restores clarity and peace.

top_7d.caption:
You have been fighting the wrong thing.

The thoughts about her are not the problem. The replays, the loops, the checking her profile at 11pm — those are not the root. Those are the symptom. And you have been spending all your energy trying to kill symptoms while the root sits untouched.

This is why the thoughts keep coming back.

You block her, and a week later you are back on the page. You journal it out, and the loop starts again by morning. You pray against it, and it returns by Thursday. Not because you are weak. Because you are fighting the output and leaving the source running.

The source is idolatry.

Somewhere underneath the obsession, you gave her a role that only God can fill. Source of worth. Proof of value. Evidence that you are enough. And until that displacement is addressed, the thoughts will keep generating because the root is still being fed.

Kill the idolatry and the thoughts starve.

Not suppress them. Not white-knuckle through them. Starve them. Remove the thing feeding them at the root, and the symptom stops having anything to run on.

This is not a thought management problem. It is a worship problem. And worship problems have worship solutions.

Understanding this is one thing.
Rewiring the thought patterns that were built around that idol…
That’s where most Christian men stay stuck.

They know the truth.
But they don’t know how to retrain their mind in real time.
So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak…

If you’re tired of the negative thought spirals…
And tomorrow night you want to look back at this season and genuinely call it a blessing…

I help Christian businessmen uncover what God is teaching them through 1:1 coaching so 
they can move from heartbroken to peaceful, hurt to grateful,
by rebuilding their purpose and drive in Christ in only 60 minutes. 

Comment “NEXT CHAPTER”
and I’ll DM you the first step. A free video I made explaining
why you can’t stop thinking about her.

The painful truth that follows will hurt. But it changed my life and if you let it, it will change yours too.

If the vision that once fueled you feels flat…
If you’re still performing at work, but it feels meaningless…

You need to hear this.
Most businessmen don’t lose their edge because they’re tired.
They lose it because something collapsed.

And in your case…
It wasn’t just the relationship.
It was the future you built around her.

You didn’t just love her.
You built a world in your head.

The marriage.
The family.
The shared wins.
The reason you were grinding so hard.

Your business wasn’t just about impact.
It wasn’t just about stewardship.
It was about building something worthy of her.

Protecting her.
Providing for her.
Becoming the man she would never leave.

And when she walked away…
It wasn’t just emotional pain.
It was the death of the future you were working toward.

And when the future dies,
so does the fuel.
That’s why the vision feels flat.
Because the vision isn’t rooted in the Kingdom.

It is still rooted in her.

Jesus said:

“But seek first the kingdom of God and His righteousness,
and all these things will be added to you.”
— Matthew 6:33

You didn’t seek first the Kingdom.
You sought first:
– The relationship
– The approval
– The security

That’s why when that imagined future collapsed,
your ambition collapsed with it.
That’s not burnout.

That’s misplaced worship.

Understanding this is one thing.
Rewiring the thought patterns that were built around that idol…
That’s where most Christian men stay stuck.

They know the truth.
But they don’t know how to retrain their mind in real time.

So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak…

If you’re tired of the negative thought spirals…

I help Christian businessmen uncover what God is teaching them through 1:1 coaching so
they can move from heartbroken to peaceful and hurt to grateful,
by rebuilding their purpose and drive in Christ in only 60 minutes.

Comment “NEXT CHAPTER”
and I’ll DM you the first step. A free video I made explaining
why you can’t stop thinking about her.

You think the more you love her, the better the relationship will be, but that hasn’t worked because that’s not God’s design. On the contrary, it’s actually the biggest danger to a christian marriage.

Not dangerous in an obvious way. Dangerous because they give everything. They prioritize her above everything. They make her their peace, their security, their purpose. 

And they call it love.
But all she feels is fear.

Why?

Because when you make a woman your foundation, you’re hiring her. You’ve given her the job of being your God. And no human being was built to carry the weight of being someone’s everything… She will feel it even if she can’t name it. The suffocating pressure of knowing that your emotional state rises and falls with whether she’s happy.

Men wonder why the most devoted partners get left. It’s because devotion aimed at the wrong target destroys the thing it’s trying to protect.

Here’s the biblical truth: 

She was never meant to be your source. She’s a gift from your source.
And the moment you start valuing the gift more than the one who gave it, you’ve lost sight of the target, and eventually, you’ll lose the gift too.

So then what does a man built to love do? 

“Monogamy to God” is the framework that fixes this. It means that your deepest loyalty, your identity, your security… none of that lives in her. 

It lives in God. 

Which means she can disappoint you without destroying you. She can push back, or be distant, or go through her own hard season… and you remain sturdy. Focused on providing her the safety of knowing that you will love her, even when she is scared.

Understanding this is one thing.
Breaking the old patterns is where most Christian men stay stuck.

So if you’ve been struggling to save your marriage, and you want to learn how to turn this hard season into the fuel to deepen your relationship with your wife…

I help Christian men understand what God wants them to learn through one-on-one coaching, so they 
can feel at peace during conflict, 
feel grateful for their wife’s emotions, and never live in fear of being left again.

But all she feels is fear.

Why?

Because when you make a woman your foundation, you’re hiring her. You’ve given her the job of being your God. And no human being was built to carry the weight of being someone’s everything… She will feel it even if she can’t name it. The suffocating pressure of knowing that your emotional state rises and falls with whether she’s happy.

Men wonder why the most devoted partners get left. It’s because devotion aimed at the wrong target destroys the thing it’s trying to protect.

Here’s the biblical truth: 

She was never meant to be your source. She’s a gift from your source.
And the moment you start valuing the gift more than the one who gave it, you’ve lost sight of the target, and eventually, you’ll lose the gift too.

So then what does a man built to love do? 

“Monogamy to God” is the framework that fixes this. It means that your deepest loyalty, your identity, your security… none of that lives in her. 

It lives in God. 

Which means she can disappoint you without destroying you. She can push back, or be distant, or go through her own hard season… and you remain sturdy. Focused on providing her the safety of knowing that you will love her, even when she is scared.

Understanding this is one thing.
Breaking the old patterns is where most Christian men stay stuck.

So if you’ve been struggling to save your marriage, and you want to learn how to turn this hard season into the fuel to deepen your relationship with your wife…

I help Christian men understand what God wants them to learn through one-on-one coaching, so they 
can feel at peace during conflict, 
feel grateful for their wife’s emotions, and never live in fear of being left again.

Here’s the biblical truth: 

She was never meant to be your source. She’s a gift from your source.
And the moment you start valuing the gift more than the one who gave it, you’ve lost sight of the target, and eventually, you’ll lose the gift too.

So then what does a man built to love do? 

“Monogamy to God” is the framework that fixes this. It means that your deepest loyalty, your identity, your security… none of that lives in her. 

It lives in God. 

Which means she can disappoint you without destroying you. She can push back, or be distant, or go through her own hard season… and you remain sturdy. Focused on providing her the safety of knowing that you will love her, even when she is scared.

Understanding this is one thing.
Breaking the old patterns is where most Christian men stay stuck.

So if you’ve been struggling to save your marriage, and you want to learn how to turn this hard season into the fuel to deepen your relationship with your wife…

I help Christian men understand what God wants them to learn through one-on-one coaching, so they 
can feel at peace during conflict, 
feel grateful for their wife’s emotions, and never live in fear of being left again.

You have been fighting the wrong thing.

The thoughts about her are not the problem. The replays, the loops, the checking her profile at 11pm — those are not the root. Those are the symptom. And you have been spending all your energy trying to kill symptoms while the root sits untouched.

This is why the thoughts keep coming back.

You block her, and a week later you are back on the page. You journal it out, and the loop starts again by morning. You pray against it, and it returns by Thursday. Not because you are weak. Because you are fighting the output and leaving the source running.

The source is idolatry.

Somewhere underneath the obsession, you gave her a role that only God can fill. Source of worth. Proof of value. Evidence that you are enough. And until that displacement is addressed, the thoughts will keep generating because the root is still being fed.

Kill the idolatry and the thoughts starve.

Not suppress them. Not white-knuckle through them. Starve them. Remove the thing feeding them at the root, and the symptom stops having anything to run on.

This is not a thought management problem. It is a worship problem. And worship problems have worship solutions.

Understanding this is one thing.
Rewiring the thought patterns that were built around that idol…
That’s where most Christian men stay stuck.

They know the truth.
But they don’t know how to retrain their mind in real time.
So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak…

If you’re tired of the negative thought spirals…

Most men are trying to escape the very thing God is using to build them.

They treat pain like a problem to be solved.

They want it gone. They pray it away. They distract it away. And when it is still there on Tuesday morning, they conclude that something is broken, either in them or in God’s plan.

Here is what no one told you. The pain is not the problem. The pain is the equipment.

Think about a gym. You do not walk in hoping the weights feel lighter this week. You show up because resistance is the only thing that builds anything. Pull the weight. Feel the burn. Walk out stronger. That is not a punishment. That is the mechanism.

That is how strength works. And you think God operates differently?

When God allows heartbreak, loss, or a season that feels like silence, He is not withholding comfort from you. He is offering you a bar to press.

You have a choice every single morning. Go to the gym or go to the bar. Lift the weight of the cross or buy yourself another round of comfort and stay exactly the same.

“Skinny fat” is real. A man who avoids hard training but does not visibly look broken. Functioning. Fine. But soft underneath.

That is what spiritual avoidance produces. A man who looks fine, until life hits him.

The men who come out of painful seasons with clarity, with character, with settled confidence did not get lucky. They went to the gym. They let the pain do what pain is designed to do.

You cannot get spiritually strong on a comfortable plan.

So if your chest is heavy right now, good. That means there is weight on the bar.

“What then shall we say to these things? If God is for us, who can be against us?” Romans 8:31

You are not lifting alone.

If you are done numbing and ready to build, type I’ll lift.

And follow this page if you want your feed to strengthen your faith, not soften it.

Here is what no one told you. The pain is not the problem. The pain is the equipment.

Think about a gym. You do not walk in hoping the weights feel lighter this week. You show up because resistance is the only thing that builds anything. Pull the weight. Feel the burn. Walk out stronger. That is not a punishment. That is the mechanism.

That is how strength works. And you think God operates differently?

When God allows heartbreak, loss, or a season that feels like silence, He is not withholding comfort from you. He is offering you a bar to press.

You have a choice every single morning. Go to the gym or go to the bar. Lift the weight of the cross or buy yourself another round of comfort and stay exactly the same.

“Skinny fat” is real. A man who avoids hard training but does not visibly look broken. Functioning. Fine. But soft underneath.

That is what spiritual avoidance produces. A man who looks fine, until life hits him.

The men who come out of painful seasons with clarity, with character, with settled confidence did not get lucky. They went to the gym. They let the pain do what pain is designed to do.

You cannot get spiritually strong on a comfortable plan.

So if your chest is heavy right now, good. That means there is weight on the bar.

“What then shall we say to these things? If God is for us, who can be against us?” Romans 8:31

You are not lifting alone.

If you are done numbing and ready to build, type I’ll lift.

And follow this page if you want your feed to strengthen your faith, not soften it.

The painful truth that follows will hurt. But it changed my life and if you let it, it will change yours too.

If the vision that once fueled you feels flat…
If you’re still performing at work, but it feels meaningless…

You need to hear this.
Most businessmen don’t lose their edge because they’re tired.
They lose it because something collapsed.

And in your case…
It wasn’t just the relationship.
It was the future you built around her.

You didn’t just love her.
You built a world in your head.

The marriage.
The family.
The shared wins.
The reason you were grinding so hard.

Your business wasn’t just about impact.
It wasn’t just about stewardship.
It was about building something worthy of her.

Protecting her.
Providing for her.
Becoming the man she would never leave.

And when she walked away…
It wasn’t just emotional pain.
It was the death of the future you were working toward.

And when the future dies,
so does the fuel.
That’s why the vision feels flat.
Because the vision isn’t rooted in the Kingdom.

It is still rooted in her.

Jesus said:

“But seek first the kingdom of God and His righteousness,
and all these things will be added to you.”
— Matthew 6:33

You didn’t seek first the Kingdom.
You sought first:
– The relationship
– The approval
– The security

That’s why when that imagined future collapsed,
your ambition collapsed with it.
That’s not burnout.

That’s misplaced worship.

Understanding this is one thing.
Rewiring the thought patterns that were built around that idol…
That’s where most Christian men stay stuck.

They know the truth.
But they don’t know how to retrain their mind in real time.

So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak…

If you’re tired of the negative thought spirals…

I help Christian businessmen uncover what God is teaching them through 1:1 coaching so
they can move from heartbroken to peaceful and hurt to grateful,
by rebuilding their purpose and drive in Christ in only 60 minutes.

top_posts.caption:
If you’re suffering after heartbreak, this is how you get peace. 
Let me explain. 

You wake up without urgency.
You move through the day without sharpness.
You’re productive, but not driven.

You pray, but it feels distant.
You work, but it feels heavy.
You train, but it feels mechanical.

And underneath it all is the same question you don’t say out loud:
“What am I actually moving toward?”

Most high-level men think this came from heartbreak.
That losing her is what broke them.

But that’s not the real loss.

What actually disappeared was the purpose, direction, and identity you unknowingly built around another human instead of God.

She didn’t just matter to you.
She became the reference point for your future.

And when that collapsed, so did the internal compass that used to pull you out of bed with clarity.

So you do what disciplined men do.

You stay busy.
You double down.
You stay productive.

And it works, briefly.

Until the quiet returns.
Until the drive doesn’t.
Until the peace that should meet you at night never shows up.

Here’s the truth most men never hear:

Healing doesn’t come from time.
It comes from realignment.

From letting the version of you that attached purpose to a person die,
so God can rebuild your identity on something unshakable.

That’s the work I do.

I help high-level Christian businessmen who don’t have the luxury of drifting rebuild their purpose in Christ,
so mornings start with direction,
your energy finally matches the responsibility you carry,
and you fall asleep with peace, knowing you lived that day the way you were meant to.

I recorded a 3-minute video breaking down the exact pattern I kept seeing after coaching men through this season,
why the thoughts keep looping,
and the shift that restores clarity and peace.

Comment “NEXT CHAPTER” and I’ll send it to you.

You have been fighting the wrong thing.

The thoughts about her are not the problem. The replays, the loops, the checking her profile at 11pm — those are not the root. Those are the symptom. And you have been spending all your energy trying to kill symptoms while the root sits untouched.

This is why the thoughts keep coming back.

You block her, and a week later you are back on the page. You journal it out, and the loop starts again by morning. You pray against it, and it returns by Thursday. Not because you are weak. Because you are fighting the output and leaving the source running.

The source is idolatry.

Somewhere underneath the obsession, you gave her a role that only God can fill. Source of worth. Proof of value. Evidence that you are enough. And until that displacement is addressed, the thoughts will keep generating because the root is still being fed.

Kill the idolatry and the thoughts starve.

Not suppress them. Not white-knuckle through them. Starve them. Remove the thing feeding them at the root, and the symptom stops having anything to run on.

This is not a thought management problem. It is a worship problem. And worship problems have worship solutions.

Understanding this is one thing.
Rewiring the thought patterns that were built around that idol…
That’s where most Christian men stay stuck.

They know the truth.
But they don’t know how to retrain their mind in real time.
So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak…

If you’re tired of the negative thought spirals…
And tomorrow night you want to look back at this season and genuinely call it a blessing…

I help Christian businessmen uncover what God is teaching them through 1:1 coaching so 
they can move from heartbroken to peaceful, hurt to grateful,
by rebuilding their purpose and drive in Christ in only 60 minutes.

and I’ll DM you the first step. A free video I made explaining
why you can’t stop thinking about her.

You’re not heartbroken because of the reason you think… And that’s why it feels like you can’t move on.
In 30 seconds I’ll explain biblically why you’re stuck, and how to actually start feeling peace again. 

The pain hits like a truck—and it stays.
You pray. You journal. You cry.

But the fog doesn’t lift.

I used to think the pain came from heartbreak.
I was wrong.

Let me show you the Biblical Truth that flipped everything:

“Now listen, you who say, ‘Today or tomorrow we will go to this or that city…’
Why, you do not even know what will happen tomorrow.
What is your life? You are a mist that appears for a little while and then vanishes.

Instead, you ought to say, ‘If it is the Lord’s will…’”
— James 4:13–15

You thought the pain came from what happened.
But what if it came from the story you wrote…
That God never signed?

The person you thought you’d marry.
The career you assumed was yours.
The future you believed God promised.

That version of life felt real.
But that future was only real in your imagination. Your best guess at what could be.
And when God chose a better future than the one you imagined…
You didn’t just lose what you wanted.

You lost your faith in His plan, and it was that mistake that stole your peace.
That’s why James says:

Don’t say, “I’ll do this or that.”
Say, “If it’s the Lord’s will.”

Surrender isn’t weakness.
It’s oxygen.

It’s stepping into the moment God is actually in…
Not the one you hoped He’d bless.

Understanding this is one thing.
Breaking the pattern is where most men stay stuck.

So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak and this helped you…

and you want to stop the negative thought spirals, break soul-ties, 
and actually be able to remember this heartbreak experience as the greatest gift you’ve ever received…

I help Christian businessmen understand what God wants them to learn
through one-on-one coaching, so they can feel grateful the relationship ended,
and never feel the pain of heartbreak again.

Comment “NEXT CHAPTER”
and I’II DM you the reason you can’t stop thinking about her no matter how hard you pray.

You might want to punch me in the face for saying this…
But if you’re still hurting, you need to read every word of this.
Because most people aren’t suffering for the reason they think.

Not after the breakup.
Not after the job loss.
Not even after the dream collapsed.

You pray. You journal. You cry.

But the fog doesn’t lift.

I used to think the pain came from heartbreak.

If you’re suffering after heartbreak, this is how you get peace. 
Let me explain. 

You wake up without urgency.
You move through the day without sharpness.
You’re productive, but not driven.

You pray, but it feels distant.
You work, but it feels heavy.
You train, but it feels mechanical.

And underneath it all is the same question you don’t say out loud:
“What am I actually moving toward?”

Most high-level men think this came from heartbreak.
That losing her is what broke them.

But that’s not the real loss.

What actually disappeared was the purpose, direction, and identity you unknowingly built around another human instead of God.

She didn’t just matter to you.
She became the reference point for your future.

And when that collapsed, so did the internal compass that used to pull you out of bed with clarity.

So you do what disciplined men do.

You stay busy.
You double down.
You stay productive.

And it works, briefly.

Until the quiet returns.
Until the drive doesn’t.
Until the peace that should meet you at night never shows up.

Here’s the truth most men never hear:

Healing doesn’t come from time.
It comes from realignment.

From letting the version of you that attached purpose to a person die,
so God can rebuild your identity on something unshakable.

That’s the work I do.

I help high-level Christian businessmen who don’t have the luxury of drifting rebuild their purpose in Christ,
so mornings start with direction,
your energy finally matches the responsibility you carry,
and you fall asleep with peace, knowing you lived that day the way you were meant to.

I recorded a 3-minute video breaking down the exact pattern I kept seeing after coaching men through this season,
why the thoughts keep looping,
and the shift that restores clarity and peace.

You have been fighting the wrong thing.

The thoughts about her are not the problem. The replays, the loops, the checking her profile at 11pm — those are not the root. Those are the symptom. And you have been spending all your energy trying to kill symptoms while the root sits untouched.

This is why the thoughts keep coming back.

You block her, and a week later you are back on the page. You journal it out, and the loop starts again by morning. You pray against it, and it returns by Thursday. Not because you are weak. Because you are fighting the output and leaving the source running.

The source is idolatry.

Somewhere underneath the obsession, you gave her a role that only God can fill. Source of worth. Proof of value. Evidence that you are enough. And until that displacement is addressed, the thoughts will keep generating because the root is still being fed.

Kill the idolatry and the thoughts starve.

Not suppress them. Not white-knuckle through them. Starve them. Remove the thing feeding them at the root, and the symptom stops having anything to run on.

This is not a thought management problem. It is a worship problem. And worship problems have worship solutions.

Understanding this is one thing.
Rewiring the thought patterns that were built around that idol…
That’s where most Christian men stay stuck.

They know the truth.
But they don’t know how to retrain their mind in real time.
So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak…

If you’re tired of the negative thought spirals…
And tomorrow night you want to look back at this season and genuinely call it a blessing…

I help Christian businessmen uncover what God is teaching them through 1:1 coaching so 
they can move from heartbroken to peaceful, hurt to grateful,
by rebuilding their purpose and drive in Christ in only 60 minutes.

If the vision that once fueled you feels flat…

It wasn’t just the relationship.

You built a world in your head.

The marriage.
The family.

The reason you were grinding so hard.

It wasn’t just about stewardship.
It was about building something worthy of her.

Protecting her.
Providing for her.
Becoming the man she would never leave.

It wasn’t just emotional pain.
It was the death of the future you were working toward.

And when the future dies,
so does the fuel.
That’s why the vision feels flat.
Because the vision isn’t rooted in the Kingdom.

It is still rooted in her.

You sought first:

– The approval
– The security

That’s why when that imagined future collapsed,
your ambition collapsed with it.
That’s not burnout.

That’s misplaced worship.

You’re not heartbroken because of the reason you think… And that’s why it feels like you can’t move on.
In 30 seconds I’ll explain biblically why you’re stuck, and how to actually start feeling peace again. 

The pain hits like a truck—and it stays.
You pray. You journal. You cry.

But the fog doesn’t lift.

I used to think the pain came from heartbreak.
I was wrong.

Let me show you the Biblical Truth that flipped everything:

“Now listen, you who say, ‘Today or tomorrow we will go to this or that city…’
Why, you do not even know what will happen tomorrow.
What is your life? You are a mist that appears for a little while and then vanishes.

Instead, you ought to say, ‘If it is the Lord’s will…’”
— James 4:13–15

You thought the pain came from what happened.
But what if it came from the story you wrote…
That God never signed?

The person you thought you’d marry.
The career you assumed was yours.
The future you believed God promised.

That version of life felt real.
But that future was only real in your imagination. Your best guess at what could be.
And when God chose a better future than the one you imagined…
You didn’t just lose what you wanted.

You lost your faith in His plan, and it was that mistake that stole your peace.
That’s why James says:

Don’t say, “I’ll do this or that.”
Say, “If it’s the Lord’s will.”

Surrender isn’t weakness.
It’s oxygen.

It’s stepping into the moment God is actually in…
Not the one you hoped He’d bless.

Understanding this is one thing.
Breaking the pattern is where most men stay stuck.

and you want to stop the negative thought spirals, break soul-ties,

If you’re suffering after heartbreak, this is how you get peace. 
Let me explain. 

You wake up without urgency.
You move through the day without sharpness.
You’re productive, but not driven.

You pray, but it feels distant.
You work, but it feels heavy.
You train, but it feels mechanical.

And underneath it all is the same question you don’t say out loud:
“What am I actually moving toward?”

Most high-level men think this came from heartbreak.
That losing her is what broke them.

But that’s not the real loss.

What actually disappeared was something subtle, but vicious. I bet this sounds familiar…

She didn’t just matter to you.
She became the reference point for your future.

And when that collapsed, so did the internal compass that used to pull you out of bed with clarity.

So you do what disciplined men do.

You stay busy.
You double down.
You stay productive.

And it works, briefly.

Until the quiet returns.
Until the drive doesn’t.
Until the peace that should meet you at night never shows up.

Here’s the truth most men never hear: The pain you’re feeling comes from 
building your purpose, direction, and identity unknowingly 
around another human instead of God.

Which means healing won’t come from time.
It will come from realignment.

From letting the version of you that attached purpose to a person die,
so God can rebuild your identity on something unshakable.

So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak and this helped you...
and you want to stop the negative thought spirals, break soul-ties, 
and actually be able to remember this heartbreak experience 
as the greatest gift you’ve ever received…

I help Christian businessmen making 6-7 figures understand what God wants them to learn 
through one-on-one coaching, so then can feel grateful the relationship ended, 
and never feel the pain of heartbreak again.

So you do what disciplined men do.

You stay busy.
You double down.
You stay productive.

And it works, briefly.

Until the quiet returns.
Until the drive doesn’t.
Until the peace that should meet you at night never shows up.

Here’s the truth most men never hear:

Healing doesn’t come from time.
It comes from realignment.

From letting the version of you that attached purpose to a person die,
so God can rebuild your identity on something unshakable.

That’s the work I do.

I help high-level Christian businessmen who don’t have the luxury of drifting rebuild their purpose in Christ,
so mornings start with direction,
your energy finally matches the responsibility you carry,
and you fall asleep with peace, knowing you lived that day the way you were meant to.

I recorded a 3-minute video breaking down the exact pattern I kept seeing after coaching men through this season,
why the thoughts keep looping,
and the shift that restores clarity and peace.

You think the more you love her, the better the relationship will be, but that hasn’t worked because that’s not God’s design. On the contrary, it’s actually the biggest danger to a christian marriage.

Not dangerous in an obvious way. Dangerous because they give everything. They prioritize her above everything. They make her their peace, their security, their purpose. 

And they call it love.
But all she feels is fear.

Why?

Because when you make a woman your foundation, you’re hiring her. You’ve given her the job of being your God. And no human being was built to carry the weight of being someone’s everything… She will feel it even if she can’t name it. The suffocating pressure of knowing that your emotional state rises and falls with whether she’s happy.

Men wonder why the most devoted partners get left. It’s because devotion aimed at the wrong target destroys the thing it’s trying to protect.

Here’s the biblical truth: 

She was never meant to be your source. She’s a gift from your source.
And the moment you start valuing the gift more than the one who gave it, you’ve lost sight of the target, and eventually, you’ll lose the gift too.

So then what does a man built to love do? 

“Monogamy to God” is the framework that fixes this. It means that your deepest loyalty, your identity, your security… none of that lives in her. 

It lives in God. 

Which means she can disappoint you without destroying you. She can push back, or be distant, or go through her own hard season… and you remain sturdy. Focused on providing her the safety of knowing that you will love her, even when she is scared.

Understanding this is one thing.
Breaking the old patterns is where most Christian men stay stuck.

So if you’ve been struggling to save your marriage, and you want to learn how to turn this hard season into the fuel to deepen your relationship with your wife…

I help Christian men understand what God wants them to learn through one-on-one coaching, so they 
can feel at peace during conflict, 
feel grateful for their wife’s emotions, and never live in fear of being left again.

You think the more you love her, the better the relationship will be, but that hasn’t worked because that’s not God’s design. On the contrary, it’s actually the biggest danger to a christian marriage.

Not dangerous in an obvious way. Dangerous because they give everything. They prioritize her above everything. They make her their peace, their security, their purpose. 

And they call it love.
But all she feels is fear.

Why?

Because when you make a woman your foundation, you’re hiring her. You’ve given her the job of being your God. And no human being was built to carry the weight of being someone’s everything… She will feel it even if she can’t name it. The suffocating pressure of knowing that your emotional state rises and falls with whether she’s happy.

Men wonder why the most devoted partners get left. It’s because devotion aimed at the wrong target destroys the thing it’s trying to protect.

Here’s the biblical truth: 

She was never meant to be your source. She’s a gift from your source.
And the moment you start valuing the gift more than the one who gave it, you’ve lost sight of the target, and eventually, you’ll lose the gift too.

So then what does a man built to love do? 

“Monogamy to God” is the framework that fixes this. It means that your deepest loyalty, your identity, your security… none of that lives in her. 

It lives in God. 

Which means she can disappoint you without destroying you. She can push back, or be distant, or go through her own hard season… and you remain sturdy. Focused on providing her the safety of knowing that you will love her, even when she is scared.

Understanding this is one thing.
Breaking the old patterns is where most Christian men stay stuck.

So if you’ve been struggling to save your marriage, and you want to learn how to turn this hard season into the fuel to deepen your relationship with your wife…

I help Christian men understand what God wants them to learn through one-on-one coaching, so they 
can feel at peace during conflict, 
feel grateful for their wife’s emotions, and never live in fear of being left again.

You have been fighting the wrong thing.

The thoughts about her are not the problem. The replays, the loops, the checking her profile at 11pm — those are not the root. Those are the symptom. And you have been spending all your energy trying to kill symptoms while the root sits untouched.

This is why the thoughts keep coming back.

You block her, and a week later you are back on the page. You journal it out, and the loop starts again by morning. You pray against it, and it returns by Thursday. Not because you are weak. Because you are fighting the output and leaving the source running.

The source is idolatry.

Somewhere underneath the obsession, you gave her a role that only God can fill. Source of worth. Proof of value. Evidence that you are enough. And until that displacement is addressed, the thoughts will keep generating because the root is still being fed.

Kill the idolatry and the thoughts starve.

Not suppress them. Not white-knuckle through them. Starve them. Remove the thing feeding them at the root, and the symptom stops having anything to run on.

This is not a thought management problem. It is a worship problem. And worship problems have worship solutions.

Understanding this is one thing.
Rewiring the thought patterns that were built around that idol…
That’s where most Christian men stay stuck.

They know the truth.
But they don’t know how to retrain their mind in real time.
So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak…

If you’re tired of the negative thought spirals…
And tomorrow night you want to look back at this season and genuinely call it a blessing…

I help Christian businessmen uncover what God is teaching them through 1:1 coaching so 
they can move from heartbroken to peaceful, hurt to grateful,
by rebuilding their purpose and drive in Christ in only 60 minutes.

Most men are trying to escape the very thing God is using to build them.

They treat pain like a problem to be solved.

They want it gone. They pray it away. They distract it away. And when it is still there on Tuesday morning, they conclude that something is broken, either in them or in God’s plan.

Here is what no one told you. The pain is not the problem. The pain is the equipment.

Think about a gym. You do not walk in hoping the weights feel lighter this week. You show up because resistance is the only thing that builds anything. Pull the weight. Feel the burn. Walk out stronger. That is not a punishment. That is the mechanism.

That is how strength works. And you think God operates differently?

When God allows heartbreak, loss, or a season that feels like silence, He is not withholding comfort from you. He is offering you a bar to press.

You have a choice every single morning. Go to the gym or go to the bar. Lift the weight of the cross or buy yourself another round of comfort and stay exactly the same.

“Skinny fat” is real. A man who avoids hard training but does not visibly look broken. Functioning. Fine. But soft underneath.

That is what spiritual avoidance produces. A man who looks fine, until life hits him.

The men who come out of painful seasons with clarity, with character, with settled confidence did not get lucky. They went to the gym. They let the pain do what pain is designed to do.

You cannot get spiritually strong on a comfortable plan.

So if your chest is heavy right now, good. That means there is weight on the bar.

“What then shall we say to these things? If God is for us, who can be against us?” Romans 8:31

You are not lifting alone.

If you are done numbing and ready to build, type I’ll lift.

And follow this page if you want your feed to strengthen your faith, not soften it.

When God allows heartbreak, loss, or a season that feels like silence, He is not withholding comfort from you. He is offering you a bar to press.

You have a choice every single morning. Go to the gym or go to the bar. Lift the weight of the cross or buy yourself another round of comfort and stay exactly the same.

“Skinny fat” is real. A man who avoids hard training but does not visibly look broken. Functioning. Fine. But soft underneath.

That is what spiritual avoidance produces. A man who looks fine, until life hits him.

The men who come out of painful seasons with clarity, with character, with settled confidence did not get lucky. They went to the gym. They let the pain do what pain is designed to do.

You cannot get spiritually strong on a comfortable plan.

So if your chest is heavy right now, good. That means there is weight on the bar.

“What then shall we say to these things? If God is for us, who can be against us?” Romans 8:31

You are not lifting alone.

If you are done numbing and ready to build, type I’ll lift.

And follow this page if you want your feed to strengthen your faith, not soften it.

The painful truth that follows will hurt. But it changed my life and if you let it, it will change yours too.

If the vision that once fueled you feels flat…
If you’re still performing at work, but it feels meaningless…

You need to hear this.
Most businessmen don’t lose their edge because they’re tired.
They lose it because something collapsed.

And in your case…
It wasn’t just the relationship.
It was the future you built around her.

You didn’t just love her.
You built a world in your head.

The marriage.
The family.
The shared wins.
The reason you were grinding so hard.

Your business wasn’t just about impact.
It wasn’t just about stewardship.
It was about building something worthy of her.

Protecting her.
Providing for her.
Becoming the man she would never leave.

And when she walked away…
It wasn’t just emotional pain.
It was the death of the future you were working toward.

And when the future dies,
so does the fuel.
That’s why the vision feels flat.
Because the vision isn’t rooted in the Kingdom.

It is still rooted in her.

Jesus said:

“But seek first the kingdom of God and His righteousness,
and all these things will be added to you.”
— Matthew 6:33

You didn’t seek first the Kingdom.
You sought first:
– The relationship
– The approval
– The security

That’s why when that imagined future collapsed,
your ambition collapsed with it.
That’s not burnout.

That’s misplaced worship.

Understanding this is one thing.
Rewiring the thought patterns that were built around that idol…
That’s where most Christian men stay stuck.

They know the truth.
But they don’t know how to retrain their mind in real time.

So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak…

If you’re tired of the negative thought spirals…

I help Christian businessmen uncover what God is teaching them through 1:1 coaching so
they can move from heartbroken to peaceful and hurt to grateful,
by rebuilding their purpose and drive in Christ in only 60 minutes.
