The painful truth that follows will hurt. But it changed my life and if you let it, it will change yours too.

If the vision that once fueled you feels flat…
If you’re still performing at work, but it feels meaningless…

You need to hear this.
Most businessmen don’t lose their edge because they’re tired.
They lose it because something collapsed.

And in your case…
It wasn’t just the relationship.
It was the future you built around her.

You didn’t just love her.
You built a world in your head.

The marriage.
The family.
The shared wins.
The reason you were grinding so hard.

Your business wasn’t just about impact.
It wasn’t just about stewardship.
It was about building something worthy of her.

Protecting her.
Providing for her.
Becoming the man she would never leave.

And when she walked away…
It wasn’t just emotional pain.
It was the death of the future you were working toward.

And when the future dies,
so does the fuel.
That’s why the vision feels flat.
Because the vision isn’t rooted in the Kingdom.

It is still rooted in her.

Jesus said:

“But seek first the kingdom of God and His righteousness,
and all these things will be added to you.”
— Matthew 6:33

You didn’t seek first the Kingdom.
You sought first:
– The relationship
– The approval
– The security

That’s why when that imagined future collapsed,
your ambition collapsed with it.
That’s not burnout.

That’s misplaced worship.

Understanding this is one thing.
Rewiring the thought patterns that were built around that idol…
That’s where most Christian men stay stuck.

They know the truth.
But they don’t know how to retrain their mind in real time.

So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak…

If you’re tired of the negative thought spirals…

I help Christian businessmen uncover what God is teaching them through 1:1 coaching so
they can move from heartbroken to peaceful, hurt to grateful,
by rebuilding their purpose and drive in Christ in only 60 minutes.

James says :

“Now listen, you who say, ‘Today or tomorrow we will go to this or that city…’
Why, you do not even know what will happen tomorrow.
What is your life? You are a mist that appears for a little while and then vanishes.

Instead, you ought to say, ‘If it is the Lord’s will…’”
— James 4:13–15

You thought the pain came from what happened.

But it came from the story you wrote ,
that God never signed.

You assumed.
You declared.
You built a five year plan
and called it God’s will.

When He chose something different
it didn’t just break your heart.

It exposed your attachment.

Surrender isn’t weakness.

It’s oxygen.

It’s stepping into the life God is actually in ,
not the one you hoped He would bless.

They miss the version of themselves they thought they were becoming with her.

And until you kill that future
you’ll keep replaying it in your mind.

You’re not heartbroken because of the reason you think… And that’s why it feels like you can’t move on.
In 30 seconds I’ll explain biblically why you’re stuck, and how to actually start feeling peace again. 

The pain hits like a truck—and it stays.
You pray. You journal. You cry.

But the fog doesn’t lift.

I used to think the pain came from heartbreak.
I was wrong.

Let me show you the Biblical Truth that flipped everything:

“Now listen, you who say, ‘Today or tomorrow we will go to this or that city…’
Why, you do not even know what will happen tomorrow.
What is your life? You are a mist that appears for a little while and then vanishes.

Instead, you ought to say, ‘If it is the Lord’s will…’”
— James 4:13–15

You thought the pain came from what happened.
But what if it came from the story you wrote…
That God never signed?

The person you thought you’d marry.
The career you assumed was yours.
The future you believed God promised.

That version of life felt real.
But that future was only real in your imagination. Your best guess at what could be.
And when God chose a better future than the one you imagined…
You didn’t just lose what you wanted.

You lost your faith in His plan, and it was that mistake that stole your peace.
That’s why James says:

Don’t say, “I’ll do this or that.”
Say, “If it’s the Lord’s will.”

Surrender isn’t weakness.
It’s oxygen.

It’s stepping into the moment God is actually in…
Not the one you hoped He’d bless.

Understanding this is one thing.
Breaking the pattern is where most men stay stuck.

So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak and this helped you…

and you want to stop the negative thought spirals, break soul-ties, 
and actually be able to remember this heartbreak experience as the greatest gift you’ve ever received…

I help Christian businessmen understand what God wants them to learn
through one-on-one coaching, so they can feel grateful the relationship ended,
and never feel the pain of heartbreak again.

But the fog doesn’t lift.

I used to think the pain came from heartbreak.
I was wrong.

Let me show you the Biblical Truth that flipped everything:

“Now listen, you who say, ‘Today or tomorrow we will go to this or that city…’
Why, you do not even know what will happen tomorrow.
What is your life? You are a mist that appears for a little while and then vanishes.

Instead, you ought to say, ‘If it is the Lord’s will…’”
— James 4:13–15

You thought the pain came from what happened.
But what if it came from the story you wrote…
That God never signed?

The person you thought you’d marry.
The career you assumed was yours.
The future you believed God promised.

That version of life felt real.
But that future was only real in your imagination. Your best guess at what could be.
And when God chose a better future than the one you imagined…
You didn’t just lose what you wanted.

You lost your faith in His plan, and it was that mistake that stole your peace.
That’s why James says:

Don’t say, “I’ll do this or that.”
Say, “If it’s the Lord’s will.”

Surrender isn’t weakness.
It’s oxygen.

It’s stepping into the moment God is actually in…
Not the one you hoped He’d bless.

Understanding this is one thing.
Breaking the pattern is where most men stay stuck.

So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak and this helped you…

and you want to stop the negative thought spirals, break soul-ties, 
and actually be able to remember this heartbreak experience as the greatest gift you’ve ever received…

I help Christian businessmen understand what God wants them to learn
through one-on-one coaching, so they can feel grateful the relationship ended,
and never feel the pain of heartbreak again.

That God never signed?

The person you thought you’d marry.
The career you assumed was yours.
The future you believed God promised.

That version of life felt real.
But that future was only real in your imagination. Your best guess at what could be.
And when God chose a better future than the one you imagined…
You didn’t just lose what you wanted.

You lost your faith in His plan, and it was that mistake that stole your peace.
That’s why James says:

Don’t say, “I’ll do this or that.”
Say, “If it’s the Lord’s will.”

Surrender isn’t weakness.
It’s oxygen.

It’s stepping into the moment God is actually in…
Not the one you hoped He’d bless.

Understanding this is one thing.
Breaking the pattern is where most men stay stuck.

So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak and this helped you…

and you want to stop the negative thought spirals, break soul-ties, 
and actually be able to remember this heartbreak experience as the greatest gift you’ve ever received…

I help Christian businessmen understand what God wants them to learn
through one-on-one coaching, so they can feel grateful the relationship ended,
and never feel the pain of heartbreak again.

If you’re suffering after heartbreak, this is how you get peace. 
Let me explain. 

You wake up without urgency.
You move through the day without sharpness.
You’re productive, but not driven.

You pray, but it feels distant.
You work, but it feels heavy.
You train, but it feels mechanical.

And underneath it all is the same question you don’t say out loud:
“What am I actually moving toward?”

Most high-level men think this came from heartbreak.
That losing her is what broke them.

But that’s not the real loss.

What actually disappeared was something subtle, but vicious. I bet this sounds familiar…

She didn’t just matter to you.
She became the reference point for your future.

And when that collapsed, so did the internal compass that used to pull you out of bed with clarity.

So you do what disciplined men do.

You stay busy.
You double down.
You stay productive.

And it works, briefly.

Until the quiet returns.
Until the drive doesn’t.
Until the peace that should meet you at night never shows up.

Here’s the truth most men never hear: The pain you’re feeling comes from 
building your purpose, direction, and identity unknowingly 
around another human instead of God.

Which means healing won’t come from time.
It will come from realignment.

From letting the version of you that attached purpose to a person die,
so God can rebuild your identity on something unshakable.

So if you’ve been struggling to get back on track after heartbreak and this helped you...
and you want to stop the negative thought spirals, break soul-ties, 
and actually be able to remember this heartbreak experience 
as the greatest gift you’ve ever received…

I help Christian businessmen making 6-7 figures understand what God wants them to learn 
through one-on-one coaching, so then can feel grateful the relationship ended, 
and never feel the pain of heartbreak again.
